channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Nigerian woman in Canada demands husband swear oath against cheating before sponsoring visa
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Nigerian woman in Canada requires her husband to swear a public oath against cheating before she sponsors his visa, sparking online debate.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerian-woman-in-canada-demands-husband-swear-oath-against-cheating-before-sponsoring-visa/

item.description:
NESREA is investigating a suspected chemical spill on the Kaduna-Abuja Expressway after a foamy substance appeared at an accident scene. Residents warned t
Read More: https://punchng.com/nesrea-probes-suspected-chemical-spill-in-kaduna-abuja-expressway/

item.title: Boko haram recruits kidnapped children when fighters are depleted, says Bwala
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Boko Haram replenishes its ranks by abducting children from villages when fighters are depleted, says presidential aide Daniel Bwala. Learn more about the
Read More: https://punchng.com/boko-haram-recruits-kidnapped-children-when-fighters-are-depleted-says-bwala/

item.title: Talent alone won’t sustain a career, discipline is key, says Ramsey Nouah
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nollywood veteran Ramsey Nouah emphasizes that discipline and consistency are crucial for a lasting career, far more than talent alone. Learn his insights.
Read More: https://punchng.com/talent-alone-wont-sustain-a-career-discipline-is-key-says-ramsey-nouah/

item.title: Idol-free palaces greater than mosques, churches – Oluwo
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Oluwo of Iwo, Oba Abdulrosheed Akanbi, declares idol-free palaces are greater than mosques and churches, calling them true stools of God on earth.
Read More: https://punchng.com/idol-free-palaces-greater-than-mosques-churches-oluwo/

item.title: CBN: Credit To Private Sector Up By 9.37% To N83.26trn In June
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Credit to Nigeria’s private sector increased by 9.37 per cent, or N7.13 trillion, to N83.26 trillion in June 2026, compared with N76.13 trillion, in the corresponding period of last year, latest data from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) shows. New Telegraph’s analysis of the latest “Money and Cred

item.title: I want to transfer $17,000 in credit-card debt. Why did Wells Fargo offer me only a $4,000 credit limit?

item.description: “I asked why the limit was so low, but they couldn’t give me an explanation.”

item.description: For decades, neo-Nazis and far-right extremists flocked to the Austrian home where Adolf Hitler.

item.title: My second husband and I have kids from previous relationships. Should my house go to him or my children if I die first?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married.”

item.title: WASPAN challenges FCCPC’s digital lending powers at Court of Appeal
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: WASPAN also filed a motion seeking an injunction restraining the FCCPC from enforcing the DEON Regulations pending the hearing and determination of the appeal. The post WASPAN challenges FCCPC’s digital lending powers at Court of Appeal appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: CAF expands AFCON to 28 teams
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) will feature 28 teams instead of 24 starting from the 2027 tournament, according to Confederation of African Football (CAF) President Patrice Motsepe.

item.title: Ukrainians welcome new army chief but seek return of fired defence minister
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Ukraine's soldiers and civilians welcomed the appointment of Mykhailo Drapaty as the new army chief on Wednesday, but many said they still wanted former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov restored after his surprise removal sparked the largest war-related protests since Russia's 2022 invasion.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Claimed attacks come days after Houthis announced naval embargo on Saudi vessels

item.description: Alex Scott favours a move to Arsenal, Bruno Fernandes is ready to commit his future to Manchester United, Liverpool are confident they will sign Bradley Barcola, plus more.

The Special Excellence Award was presented at the associatio

item.title: Arokodare forces Wolves training to be cancelled
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Wolves striker Tolu Arokodare forces the cancellation of a training session as he refuses to leave the pitch.

item.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist

item.description: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US court sets June 2027 trial for Venezuela’s ousted Maduro
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Venezuela's ousted president Nicolas Maduro and his wife will go on trial in the United States in June 2027, a court ruled Wednesday, after the US military captured them in a stunning nighttime raid in January. item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US equity-index futures declined in early Thursday trading as investors weighed earnings from AI heavyweights for evidence that surging capital spending is translating into growth. Oil extended its rally.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to NAFDAC, the nationwide enforcement operation is holding in the six geopolitical zones and follows the closure of manufacturing facilities found to have violated the ban.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Trading Parts of $35 Billion AI Chip Deal
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Wall Street banks have started buying and selling an initial piece of a $35 billion financing package for Broadcom Inc. and Anthropic PBC’s AI infrastructure expansion, with the biggest private credit deal ever now being offered to a wider pool of investors.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil prices rose sharply in post-settlement trading Wednesday, with Brent crude oil rising near $95 a barrel after Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels said they struck two Saudi tankers.

item.title: France Records Nearly 5,800 Excess Deaths During Record Heatwave
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: French health authorities say a record-breaking heatwave last month led to 5,764 more deaths than expected, describing the excess mortality among people aged 15 and older as "unprecedented." The post France Records Nearly 5,800 Excess Deaths During Record Heatwave appeared first on Trending News.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Charity Nwakaudu, Abuja More than 80 young Nigerians smiled home with startup grants and leadership certificates on Friday as the Director-General of the Energy Commission of Nigeria (ECN), Dr. Mustapha Abdullahi, empowered them under the maiden Mustapha Abdullahi Energy Leadership Fellowship (

item.title: Minister seeks Police support to protect critical power infrastructure
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ministry of Power, in line with current reforms in the sector, has sought the support and cooperation of the Nigeria Police Force (NPF) to The post Minister seeks Police support to protect critical power infrastructure appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: NPFL: Kwara United unveil 16 new players for 2026-27 season
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kwara United have unveiled 16 new players for the 2026-27 season, DAILY POST reports.The Ilorin-based club retained only 14 players from the previous season. The Harmony Boys made the announcement during the unveiling ceremony on Wednesday at the club secretariat. The club chairman, Mr Kumbi Titiloy

item.title: NPFL: Majayogbe joins Doma United on two-year contract
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Muyiwa Majayogbe has signed a two-year contract with Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) side Doma United, DAILY POST reports. Majayogbe joined the Savannah Tigers from Nigeria National League (NNL) side Crown FC. The goalkeeper reunited with head coach Ayodeji Ayeni at Doma United. Majayogbe pla

item.title: Vitamin Well Raises $2.2 Billion From Cross-Currency Loan Deal
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Vitamin Well AB raised about $2.2 billion through a cross-currency loan offering to fund its acquisition of protein-bar maker EMPWR Nutrition Group.

item.title: Nigerian opposition jobless, lazying around – Tinubu’s aide, Bwala
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Daniel Bwala, President Bola Tinubu’s Special Adviser on Policy Communication, on Wednesday described the opposition as jobless, stressing that they are “lazying about.” Bwala made the remark while featuring on Channels Television’s Politics Today, in reaction to the United S

item.title: Moore: Joshua Won’t Escape Heavy Defeat Against Prenga
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Heavyweight contender Brandon Moore has warned Anthony Joshua that Kristian Prenga possesses the power to recreate the upset Andy Ruiz Jr. caused. Moore, who previously sparred with the Albanian fighter, stated Prenga carries genuine concussive force and could emulate Ruiz’s historic knockout

item.title: PICTORIAL: Governors, Political Leaders Attend Wike’s Daughter’s Graduation In UK
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Top political figures from across Nigeria gathered in the United Kingdom on Wednesday as Jazmyne Wike, the daughter of the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, graduated with a Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree, earning Upper Second-Class Honours from the University of Southam

item.title: Minister, IGP Agree on Taskforce to Fight Power Infrastructure Vandalism
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Minister of Power, Joseph Tegbe, has asked the Nigeria Police Force to intensify protection of the country’s power infrastructure, he told the Inspector‑General of Police, Tunji Disu, during a courtesy visit to the Force Headquarters in Abuja on Tuesday. Tegbe, who was accompanied by the Ministr

item.title: How Lawal tackled imbalance with nursing college in Zamfara North, By Suleman Tudu
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The corrective steps being taken by the Dauda Lawal's administration to address decades of imbalance by establishing a tertiary institution in Zamfara North is worthy of commendation. That institution is the College of Nursing Sciences in Zurmi LGA The post How Lawal tackled imbalance with nursing c

item.title: Delayed maritime cases raise import costs, hurt investment, SGF tells stakeholders
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Federal Government has linked the efficiency of Nigeria’s maritime justice system to the country’s trade competitiveness, warning that delays
